name: First Basin
description_short: First Basin Credit Union is a Texas-based financial cooperative serving members with banking services, operating from Odessa with multiple contact channels for member support.
description_long:
First Basin Credit Union is a state-chartered credit union incorporated under Texas law and regulated by the Texas Credit Union Department. The institution operates as a member-owned financial cooperative, which distinguishes it from traditional for-profit banks. Based in Odessa, Texas, First Basin maintains a physical branch location at 7100 E. TX Hwy 191 and serves members through both in-person and remote channels.

The organization provides standard credit union banking services accessible through online platforms, member accounts, and debit card products. Members can access 24/7 cardholder assistance and regular member services during business hours. The credit union offers account management capabilities, including the ability to update member information and access customer support for account-related questions and concerns.

As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, First Basin operates on a member-ownership model where profits are theoretically returned to members rather than shareholders. The organization emphasizes member service and satisfaction, including branch-based feedback collection and responsive customer support infrastructure with multiple contact methods including phone lines, email, and in-person service.

Based on available information, First Basin functions as a basic banking service provider without evidence of specialized lending products, investment services, or advanced financial products. The website content focuses primarily on customer service and account support rather than detailing specific deposit products, loan offerings, or rates. For consumers seeking comprehensive information about specific products, rates, or terms, direct contact with the credit union would be necessary. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online contact form for account inquiries
In-person branch banking at Odessa location
Complaint resolution process with regulatory oversight
pros: 24/7 cardholder assistance line (800-547-1759) for emergency card support
Multiple contact channels including phone, email, fax, and in-person service
Dedicated lost/stolen card hotline with both U.S. and international numbers
Member survey program demonstrating commitment to feedback and service improvement
State credit union charter with regulatory oversight by Texas Credit Union Department
Reasonable response time of two business days for online inquiries
After-hours and weekend member services line (800-322-7242)
cons: Website lacks specific information about loan products, interest rates, or deposit account details
Limited geographic presence as a local/regional credit union serving primarily Texas
Two-business-day response time for online inquiries may be slow for time-sensitive issues
No evidence of digital banking features, mobile app, or online account opening on contact page
Contact page provides no details about membership eligibility or account opening process
best_for: Texas residents seeking membership in a local credit union with personalized service
Members already established with First Basin needing customer service support
Consumers preferring credit union banking over traditional commercial banks
diagnosis: First Basin Credit Union is best for existing members in Texas seeking standard banking services and customer support, particularly those who value credit union membership and local branch access. The main caveat is that the contact page does not provide details about specific products, rates, or services, so prospective members would need to contact the credit union directly to evaluate offerings.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
